title: arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. cratericola
intro: Arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. cratericola, also known as bearberry, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include astringent and antiseptic properties due to its high content of arbutin and uva-ursi acid. These medicinal properties provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, treating urinary tract infections, and supporting kidney function, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Bearberry can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and capsules, which are commonly used to support urinary health and alleviate symptoms associated with UTIs.
